meaning: n.引座员,招待员,传达员,前驱vt.引导,展示vi.作招待员
examples: He did part-time work as an usher in a theatre.
Usher: Sorry, but I'm afraid not.
The usher bowed us into the theatre.
She will return to Usher Hall in Edinburgh for the final on 18 May 2014.
We also usher you inside the charming, egoreadjusting world of hickory golf (page 58).
